original text:
Aman Ullah with Lwin others.
Security Council issued presidential statement Myanmar complimenting criticising country over stance Rohingya crisis also gave government month "get together". 1,300- word statement read sitting President UNSC Sebastiano Cardi, Ambassador Italy, formal UNSC session Monday November 2017)
presidential statement statement made President Security Council behalf Council, adopted formal meeting Council issued official document Council.
Presidential Statement often created when United Nations Security Council cannot reach consensus prevented from passing resolution permanent member's veto, threat thereof. Such statements similar content, format, tone resolutions, legally binding. adoption Presidential Statement requires consensus, although Security Council members abstain. Statement signed sitting Security Council President.
first presidential statement years Myanmar. Security Council adopted such statements against Myanmar only three times, with last coming decade ago. Diplomats hailed move unified international stand against humanitarian crisis that been called "ethnic cleansing" U.N., U.S., France U.K.
strongly condemned attacks against Myanmar security forces carried Arakan Rohingya Salvation Army (ARSA) August then expressed "grave concern" over government's response, alleged burning villages threats villagers flee, among others.
called reform Myanmar's security justice sectors urged government work with Bangladesh allow voluntary return refugees their homes, basis October memorandum understanding between country.
panel welcomed "union enterprise mechanism" humanitarian assistance, resettlement development Rakhine.
recommended government ensure mechanism supported such return allow agencies full access, urging governments humanitarian partners special attention needs women, particular survivors sexual violence.
council said government's primary responsibility protection Myanmar's population, citizens not. statement reiterated concerns demands previously laid out: excessive military force Rakhine province, lack humanitarian access U.N., right return refugees, steps address root causes conflict. statement included most demands contained draft resolution presented last month Britain France, that measure into strong opposition from China, supporter Myanmar's former ruling junta.
said that, China indicated willing resort veto power block resolution, Beijing finally agreed statement during negotiations.
action step below Security Council resolution diplomatic significance, step above Council's previous action, "press statement," September.
statement requires U.N. Secretary General António Guterres report back Council within days Myanmar's progress addressing Council's demands. statement isn't binding resolution would carries punitive measures, such sanctions, corrective steps aren't taken.
Security Council held series meetings Myanmar since September, including briefing attended high-level officials sidelines U.N. General Assembly gathering. efforts respond forcefully have stalled despite widespread reports deaths, rapes torture Rohingya Muslim minorities, including women children, hands country's military.
Violence erupted August after Rohingya militant group attacked Myanmar security forces. Since late August, more than 600,000 Rohingya have been driven from their homes army campaign that United Nations described ethnic cleansing.
During negotiations with China, language citizenship rights watered down statement demand that Myanmar allow human rights mission into country, diplomats said.
statement calls Myanmar cooperate with United Nations encourages Antonio Guterres appoint special advisor Myanmar.
council statement agreed Guterres prepares travel Manila this week join leaders Southeast Asian (ASEAN) bloc summit.
council also welcomed Myanmar government's public support Advisory Commission Rakhine State chaired former Kofi Annan called their full implementation.
urged Antonio Guterres consider appointing special advisor Myanmar.
Since August attacks, over 604,000 Rohingya have fled Bangladesh. Some fled land, others boats over inlet Bengal.
last weeks, 4,000 refugees entered Bangladesh while four people drowned shipwreck while fleeing, officials said.
Foreign Secretary Boris Johnson said: pleased that today November) United Nations Security Council (UNSC) spoken with voice appalling situation Rakhine State, Burma. repeatedly called Burmese security forces protect civilians stop violence allow humanitarian urgently reach those need UNSC today joined that call, with this historic Presidential Statement Burma.
encouraged State Counsellor Aung making important steps forward, including establishing domestic body deliver humanitarian development assistance Rakhine, making efforts promote interfaith intercommunal harmony, including recent visit northern Rakhine. will watching closely ensure that Burmese security forces attempt frustrate these efforts.
U.K. France initially circulated proposed Security Council resolution Myanmar late October, China Russia refused engage negotiate, diplomats said. China finally agreed similar text with minor changes presidential carries less weight than resolution—and Russia followed suit.
Guterres been outspoken about Myanmar crisis urged Security Council prevent continued bloodshed curb refugee flow. Guterres will among world leaders attending Association Southeast Asian Nations (Asean) summit later this week Manila.
Rohingya refugee crisis expected issue discussion summit, attended President Donald Trump, will dispatch Secretary State Tillerson Myanmar later this month. Aung will also attend that summit.
